skill.name: flow-nexus-neural
skill.description: Train, deploy, monitor, and benchmark neural networks through the Flow Nexus MCP service.
skill.summary: Run neural network training and inference workflows through Flow Nexus.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many shell, network, and weak-cryptography patterns, but most are Markdown examples or ML terminology false positives. The confirmed risk is legitimate but elevated: users are instructed to install a third-party MCP server with npx and use an authenticated external Flow Nexus service for training and inference.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Server Installation and Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add a Flow Nexus MCP server through npx and to run registration and login commands. This is expected for the stated workflow, but it executes third-party package code and depends on the current published package version.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit user-run setup steps. They are not hidden malware, but they do execute third-party package code.

medium_findings.title: External Service and Data Processing Dependency
medium_findings.description: The skill is built around authenticated Flow Nexus services and distributed E2B sandbox training. User datasets, model inputs, and model metadata may be processed by external infrastructure, so users need data-handling review before sensitive workloads.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation repeatedly describes authenticated external service use and distributed sandbox training. No exfiltration intent is shown, but external processing is inherent.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Detections Are Mostly Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged many Ruby or shell backtick patterns, but the reviewed locations are Markdown code fences, inline terms, or JavaScript MCP examples. Only the setup commands are meaningful external command evidence.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed hits are inside Markdown examples or inline formatting, not executable Ruby code. The file is documentation, not a runnable script.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are ML Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography findings match neural-network terms and examples, including BERT, ResNet, transformer, nodes, and model descriptions. No evidence found of cryptographic functions, password hashing, ciphers, or signature verification.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found ML architecture and deployment language at the flagged lines. There is no cryptographic implementation in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The network findings identify Flow Nexus documentation, marketplace, API, and registration URLs. They are transparent resource links for the service described by the skill, not hidden callbacks or data exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in a Resources section and authentication note. They point to the named Flow Nexus service rather than an unrelated or obfuscated host.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: Review did not find instructions that claim system authority, ask the evaluator to ignore analysis rules, or try to override marketplace review. One ordinary use of the word override refers to changing a cluster topology value.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Keyword search found only a normal configuration comment. No suspicious instruction text was present in the reviewed file.

content.user_title: Train Neural Models with Flow Nexus
content.value_statement: Neural model workflows can be hard to configure across training, inference, deployment, and monitoring. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured prompts for Flow Nexus MCP workflows.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to configure feedforward, LSTM, transformer, GAN, and autoencoder model architectures.
Provides examples for single-node training, distributed training, and federated learning clusters.
Documents Flow Nexus MCP calls for prediction, template browsing, template deployment, and publishing.
Includes model management workflows for listing models, checking training status, benchmarking, and validation.
Covers troubleshooting steps for stalled training, low accuracy, and memory limits.
limitations: Requires a working Flow Nexus MCP server and Flow Nexus authentication.
Does not include local training code or datasets inside the skill package.
Example results are illustrative and do not guarantee model accuracy or performance.
Sensitive data needs independent privacy and compliance review before external processing.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Classifier
use_cases.description: Configure a small neural network, start training, and inspect expected training parameters.

use_cases.title: Scale Distributed Training
use_cases.description: Initialize a Flow Nexus cluster and coordinate worker nodes for larger neural workloads.

use_cases.title: Deploy Marketplace Templates
use_cases.description: Find a pretrained template, adjust training settings, and run model predictions.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Small Training Job
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a simple classifier.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Flow Nexus neural skill to plan a small feedforward classifier. Include architecture, training settings, tier, and monitoring steps.

prompt_templates.title: Run Inference on a Model
prompt_templates.scenario: Use an existing trained model for predictions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a Flow Nexus prediction workflow for model_id MODEL_ID using these inputs: INPUTS. Explain the expected result fields.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Distributed Cluster
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Flow Nexus distributed training cluster for DATASET and ARCHITECTURE. Include topology, node roles, training settings, and shutdown steps.

prompt_templates.title: Benchmark and Validate a Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Flow Nexus workflow to benchmark, validate, and publish MODEL_ID. Include performance checks, validation type, and marketplace readiness criteria.

output_examples.input: Plan a transformer training workflow for sentiment analysis.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step Flow Nexus plan with architecture choices, training settings, resource tier, monitoring commands, and validation checks.

output_examples.input: Show how to deploy a pretrained sentiment template.
output_examples.output: A concise deployment workflow that selects the template, adjusts training parameters, runs inference, and reviews prediction results.

output_examples.input: Troubleshoot a stalled distributed training cluster.
output_examples.output: A diagnostic checklist covering cluster status, worker health, resource limits, restart options, and safe termination.

best_practices: Start with nano or mini tiers before scaling to larger distributed jobs.
Benchmark and validate models before production deployment or marketplace publishing.
Review data sensitivity before sending datasets or inference inputs to external services.
anti_patterns: Do not use latest package installs in production without pinning and review.
Do not train on sensitive datasets before confirming Flow Nexus data handling terms.
Do not publish templates without documenting accuracy, validation data, and limitations.

faq.question: Does this skill train models locally?
faq.answer: No. It documents Flow Nexus MCP workflows that use external Flow Nexus and E2B sandbox infrastructure.

faq.question: Does it require authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill states that Flow Nexus registration and login are required for distributed training.

faq.question: Which model architectures are covered?
faq.answer: It covers feedforward, LSTM, transformer, GAN, and autoencoder examples.

faq.question: Can it deploy pretrained templates?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes template listing, deployment, rating, and publishing workflows.

faq.question: Is it safe for private medical data?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill mentions federated learning, but sensitive data still needs privacy and compliance review.

faq.question: Why is the marketplace risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but it executes third-party setup commands and depends on external authenticated services.
